records.ety: [Written also, more analogically, but less commonly, criticize.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Criticised (-s?zd); p. pr. & vb. n. Criticising.]

defs: To examine and judge as a critic; to pass literary or artistic judgment upon; as, to criticise an author; to criticise a picture.
To express one's views as to the merit or demerit of; esp., to animadvert upon; to find fault with; as, to criticise conduct.
To act as a critic; to pass literary or artistic judgment; to play the critic; -- formerly used with on or upon.
quotes: Several of these ladies, indeed, criticised upon the form of the association.

defs: To discuss the merits or demerits of a thing or person; esp., to find fault.
quotes: Cavil you may, but never criticise.
